raw_source_reread_reason: The parent batch already preserved the archive-only root role, generated mirror lineage, demotion and cleanup split, tiered archive topology, historical process snapshot, and plaque-versus-accretion drift. Existing archive-side A2-mid anchors were sufficient to narrow those surfaces without rereading deeper archive contents.
reasons_for_candidate_status: The archive-root batch is the correct entry packet for archive-side reduction after the completed sims lane.
Its strongest reusable value is boundary hygiene: archive-only role, generated mirror nonauthority, and tier-specific archive topology.
Its historical value depends on preserving demotion-first then cleanup-thinning lineage rather than a flattened archive doctrine.
Its surface-evolution value depends on preserving plaque date and later heat-dump accretion together.
reasons_for_quarantine: Root-level archive mirrors are not treated as active A2 or A1 authority.
Cleanup bookkeeping is not promoted into timeless archive doctrine.
Archive tiers are not flattened into one dump class.
Historical process snapshot is not treated as current maturity state.
This root-policy reduction does not absorb the deferred ZIP family.
